title: Numeric comptime interfaces as parameter types require undisclosed compiler changes
description: The spec shows: 'fn popcount(value: std.meta.Integer) u32'. But std.meta.Integer is defined as 'pub fn Integer(comptime T: type) void'. Using a fn(type)void as a parameter type is not valid Zig. In Zig today you would write 'fn popcount(value: anytype) u32' with an internal comptime check. The spec claims 'This is a pure standard library addition... no compiler changes required.' This claim is false. Either the parameter-type-as-constraint syntax requires new compiler support, or the examples are aspirational and the actual stdlib interface would look different. What caller observes: the spec's code examples won't compile in Zig today, and the degree of compiler change required is hidden.
